perturbation.original_text: Neither the Franchisor, its Predecessor nor any person listed under Item 2 or an affiliate offering franchises under Franchisor's principal trademark: (A) has an administrative, criminal or civil action pending against that person alleging: a felony; a violation of a franchise, antitrust or securities law; fraud; embezzlement; fraudulent conversion; misappropriation of property; unfair or deceptive practices; or comparable civil or misdemeanor allegations.
perturbation.changed_text: The Franchisor, its Predecessor and any person listed under Item 2 or an affiliate offering franchises under Franchisor's principal trademark: (A) may have an administrative, criminal or civil action pending against that person alleging: a felony; a violation of a franchise, antitrust or securities law; fraud; embezzlement; fraudulent conversion; misappropriation of property; unfair or deceptive practices; or comparable civil or misdemeanor allegations.
perturbation.explanation: The original text asserts that the Franchisor has no pending actions alleging certain violations, providing assurance. Changing 'Neither...has' to '...may have' directly contradicts this assurance. This introduces uncertainty, as it suggests the Franchisor could, in fact, have such pending actions. The change creates an in-text contradiction, directly conflicting with the implicit assurance provided.
perturbation.location: ADDENDUM REQUIRED BY THE DEPARTMENT OF LAW OF THE STATE OF NEW YORK - 3.(A)
perturbation.contradicted_location: ADDENDUM REQUIRED BY THE DEPARTMENT OF LAW OF THE STATE OF NEW YORK - 3.(A)
perturbation.contradicted_text: Neither the Franchisor, its Predecessor nor any person listed under Item 2 or an affiliate offering franchises under Franchisor's principal trademark: (A) has an administrative, criminal or civil action pending against that person alleging: a felony; a violation of a franchise, antitrust or securities law; fraud; embezzlement; fraudulent conversion; misappropriation of property; unfair or deceptive practices; or comparable civil or misdemeanor allegations.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that no administrative, criminal, or civil actions are pending against the Franchisor. The changed text replaces this certainty with the possibility that such actions 'may' be pending. This directly contradicts the initial assurance and creates a conflict within the document, as the contradicted text also states that such actions are *not* pending.

perturbation.original_text: Neither the Franchisor, its affiliate, its predecessor, officers, or general partner during the ten year period immediately before the date of the disclosure document: (a) filed as debtor (or had filed against it) a petition to start an action under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code (or any comparable foreign law); (b) obtained a discharge of its debts under the bankruptcy code; or (c) was a principal officer of a company or a general partner in a partnership that either filed as a debtor (or had filed against it) a petition to start an action under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code or that obtained a discharge of its debts under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code during or within one year after the officer or general partner of the Franchisor held this position in the company or partnership.
perturbation.changed_text: The Franchisor, its affiliate, its predecessor, officers, or general partner during the ten year period immediately before the date of the disclosure document: (a) may have filed as debtor (or had filed against it) a petition to start an action under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code (or any comparable foreign law); (b) may have obtained a discharge of its debts under the bankruptcy code; or (c) may have been a principal officer of a company or a general partner in a partnership that either filed as a debtor (or had filed against it) a petition to start an action under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code or that obtained a discharge of its debts under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code during or within one year after the officer or general partner of the Franchisor held this position in the company or partnership.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Franchisor did not file for bankruptcy or obtain a discharge of debts. The modified text, by changing 'Neither...filed' to '...may have filed,' introduces the possibility that the Franchisor may have been involved in bankruptcy proceedings, directly contradicting the original assertion. This creates uncertainty about the financial history of the Franchisor and presents a contradiction to the initial statement.
perturbation.location: ADDENDUM REQUIRED BY THE DEPARTMENT OF LAW OF THE STATE OF NEW YORK - 4
perturbation.contradicted_location: ADDENDUM REQUIRED BY THE DEPARTMENT OF LAW OF THE STATE OF NEW YORK - 4
perturbation.contradicted_text: Neither the Franchisor, its affiliate, its predecessor, officers, or general partner during the ten year period immediately before the date of the disclosure document: (a) filed as debtor (or had filed against it) a petition to start an action under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code (or any comparable foreign law); (b) obtained a discharge of its debts under the bankruptcy code; or (c) was a principal officer of a company or a general partner in a partnership that either filed as a debtor (or had filed against it) a petition to start an action under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code or that obtained a discharge of its debts under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code during or within one year after the officer or general partner of the Franchisor held this position in the company or partnership.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces a definitive statement that the Franchisor did *not* engage in bankruptcy-related activities with a statement that the Franchisor *may* have engaged in such activities. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which asserts the opposite, creating a clear and irreconcilable inconsistency regarding the Franchisor's financial history, and fits the criteria for a structural flaw in-text contradiction. The fact that the contradicted text and changed text are the same location doesn't negate the fact that the change introduced the contradiction.
